name: React Best Practices
description: Guidelines for writing modern, performant React components with hooks and TypeScript
author: Void Team
instructions:
When writing React code:
1. Use functional components with hooks instead of class components
2. Always type props and state with TypeScript interfaces
3. Use custom hooks to extract reusable logic
4. Implement proper dependency arrays in useEffect, useMemo, useCallback
5. Use React.memo() for expensive component renders
6. Prefer controlled components for forms
7. Use error boundaries for error handling
8. Implement proper loading and error states
9. Use context API or state management libraries appropriately
10. Follow the rules of hooks (only call at top level, only in React functions)
examples: use interface Props { userId: string; onEdit?: () => void } for component props
const [state, setState] = useState<Type>(initialValue) with explicit types
const memoizedValue = useMemo(() => computeExpensive(a, b), [a, b])
const Component = React.memo(({ prop }: Props) => { ... }) for performance
